excerpt.rendered: What shipped with Workbench 3.1 in 1994, and what's still useful in 2026.

content.rendered:
Workbench 3.1 shipped in 1994 with Kickstart 40.68. It was the last 'classic' release of AmigaOS in the Commodore era and the version most longtime Amiga users still consider definitive.

What's in the box

  • The familiar four-colour palette and Topaz 8 font.
  • Datatypes for transparent image, sound, and text decoding.
  • Crashed-but-recoverable multitasking via Exec.library.

The classic title bar gadgets — close, depth, zoom, sizing — are all there, and you'll find faithful reproductions of each on this site.

Guru meditations are still beautiful. Don't @ us.
